title: eriogonum allenii
intro: Eriogonum allenii, also known as Allen's buckwheat, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). It is native to the southwestern United States and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting immune function. The herbal preparations of Eriogonum allenii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
